# Last Time

::: {.incremental}
+ **File I/O**
+ **Sequential I/O**
+ **Random Access**
:::

::: notes
Brief recall: students wrote a vector to a file and read it back in the lab. Random access with fixed-size records was the last concept introduced.
:::

---

# Today's Agenda

- Introduction to Arrays
- Array Initialization
- Accessing Array Elements in Loops
- Inputting and Displaying Array Data
- Vectors vs. Arrays
- Performance — Vectors vs. Arrays *(optional)*

::: notes

:::

{{< include topic_1_intro_arrays.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_2_array_initialization.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_3_array_loops.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_4_input_display.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_5_vectors_vs_arrays.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_6_performance.qmd >}}

---

# Key Takeaways

- An array is a fixed-size, contiguous block of same-type elements on the stack — declare size with a `const int`, initialize with `= {}`
- Access elements with `array[i]`; valid indices run `0` through `size - 1` — C++ does not check bounds
- Use index-based `for` when you need the position; use range-for when you don't
- Default to `vector`; reach for an array when size is fixed at compile time or overhead must be zero

---

# Next Time

---

# Appendix

---

## Introduction to Arrays: Good Practices

---

## Introduction to Arrays: Common Mistakes

---

## Introduction to Arrays: More Examples

---

## Introduction to Arrays: Practice Problems

---

## Introduction to Arrays: AI Search Terms

---

## Introduction to Arrays: Questions for Reflection

---

## Array Initialization: Good Practices

---

## Array Initialization: Common Mistakes

---

## Array Initialization: More Examples

---

## Array Initialization: Practice Problems

---

## Array Initialization: AI Search Terms

---

## Array Initialization: Questions for Reflection

---

## Accessing Array Elements in Loops: Good Practices

---

## Accessing Array Elements in Loops: Common Mistakes

---

## Accessing Array Elements in Loops: More Examples

---

## Accessing Array Elements in Loops: Practice Problems

---

## Accessing Array Elements in Loops: AI Search Terms

---

## Accessing Array Elements in Loops: Questions for Reflection

---

## Inputting and Displaying Array Data: Good Practices

---

## Inputting and Displaying Array Data: Common Mistakes

---

## Inputting and Displaying Array Data: More Examples

---

## Inputting and Displaying Array Data: Practice Problems

---

## Inputting and Displaying Array Data: AI Search Terms

---

## Inputting and Displaying Array Data: Questions for Reflection

---

## Vectors vs. Arrays: Good Practices

---

## Vectors vs. Arrays: Common Mistakes

---

## Vectors vs. Arrays: More Examples

---

## Vectors vs. Arrays: Practice Problems

---

## Vectors vs. Arrays: AI Search Terms

---

## Vectors vs. Arrays: Questions for Reflection

---

## Performance — Vectors vs. Arrays: Good Practices

---

## Performance — Vectors vs. Arrays: Common Mistakes

---

## Performance — Vectors vs. Arrays: More Examples

---

## Performance — Vectors vs. Arrays: Practice Problems

---

## Performance — Vectors vs. Arrays: AI Search Terms

---

## Performance — Vectors vs. Arrays: Questions for Reflection
